title: Rookie cards investment shortlist + open Qs — Aug 2020
html:
TL;DR

  • Aug 24, 2020 — Yahir's working list of NBA rookie-card targets (Luka Dončić, Ja Morant, Anthony Davis, Kevin Durant, Devin Booker, Pascal Siakam, Rudy Gobert, Tyler Herro, Kobe, Hakeem Olajuwon) plus a structured open-questions-for-mentor block at the bottom — the prep doc for what becomes the Sept 23 mentor session one month later.
  • Same shape as a value investing watchlist: he's listing the names he believes have asymmetric upside, then enumerating the diligence questions (best brand to invest in, ideal grading conditions, community, whether opening packs is worth it, what the # on a card refers to).
  • Tyler Herro appears twice in the list — likely his then-favored breakout pick. Luka and Ja Morant lead — both 2018-19 / 2019-20 rookie-card boom-cycle names.
  • Confirms the 2020 09 23 quick flips or long term cards strategy split was already incubating: the question "Is sports cards opening worth it?" is exactly the quick-flips-vs-buy-and-hold dilemma he asks the mentor about a month later.
  • Appears alongside 2020 08 24 anime list (same date) — Aug 24, 2020 was a multi-list capture day at the start of Yahir's senior year.

Key fragments

"Luka Doncic / Ja Morant / Anthony Davis"

"Tyler Herro / KD / Kobe / Hakeem Alajuon / AD / Ja Morant"

"Which brand is the best to invest in, most well known? / Whats ideal conditions? / What's community like? / Is sports cards opening worth it? / What does the # refer do"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Pre-mentor research-doc evidence: Yahir doesn't show up to a mentor cold. He drafts the questions, builds the watchlist, comes prepared. Same pattern that recurs in 2021 04 10 ford osu prep todos and 2021 08 03 call ford mentor.
  • Confirms the 2020 sports-cards-as-investing chapter as a real, multi-month thread — not a passing interest. List → Questions → Mentor session → Strategy split (flips vs. holds). That's an arc worth a future synthesis page.
  • The cluster of names is canonical 2018-2020-boom-cycle (Luka, Ja, Tyler Herro), grounded in then-current nba rookies plus all-time names (Kobe, Hakeem) for the legacy-investment side. The selection itself is calibrated.
  • Pairs with the eventual Sept 23 quick flips strategy summary — that note's strategic split (200 packs/$1k flips vs longer-term holds) directly answers the questions on this list.
